title: VCP Capability Grant
description: A single-use, proof-bound authorization token minted by the Gateway after a policy `allow`. Authorizes exactly one invocation. See SPECIFICATION.md §7. A grant is audience-bound (capability_id), argument-bound (argument_hash), plan-bound (plan_hash), time-bound (expires_at), scope-bound (network/resource_scope/budget), proof-of-possession bound, and attenuable-only (may be narrowed, never widened).

subject.description: The principal on whose behalf the call is authorized, e.g. user:123.

audience.description: The exact capability_id this grant authorizes. A grant for one capability MUST NOT authorize another.

expires_at.description: Short TTL. RECOMMENDED <= 300s from mint time (§7).

max_calls.description: Single-use by default. The Gateway MUST reject reuse beyond this count (replay defense, §17 test 6).

network.description: Egress ceiling. MUST be a subset of the capability's sandbox.network.

budget.description: Spend/usage ceiling, designed for enforcement by a ledger substrate (mcp-ledger). Open cost dimensions.

proof_of_possession.description: DPoP-style key binding. The holder MUST prove possession of the key whose thumbprint is jkt; a leaked grant alone is unusable (§7).

jkt.description: JWK SHA-256 thumbprint of the holder's public key.

attenuated_from.description: OPTIONAL grant_id this grant was narrowed from. A Gateway MAY narrow scope before delegating; it MUST NOT widen (§7).

delegation_chain.description: On-behalf-of chain (§26.2): ordered authorizer -> delegate -> enforcer -> executor -> resource. Authority strictly narrows down the chain.

id.description: e.g. user:123, agent:researcher, gateway:edge-1, example.calendar, https://calendar.example.com

token_exchange.description: Per-provider credential brokering (§26.1) via RFC 8693 token exchange. The raw token is NEVER carried here — only its audience and key thumbprint, by reference.

audience.description: Resource indicator (RFC 8707) the exchanged token is bound to.

actor.description: The act-claim subject: the agent acting on behalf of the user.

credential_jkt.description: Thumbprint of the exchanged credential; the token itself is held behind the egress boundary.

attestation_ref.description: Reference to the verified environment attestation (§27) that gated this grant, when the capability required one. The full statement is NOT carried here (attest-once / reference-many).
